title: French Beans Foogath with Rice
description: South Indian-style French beans stir-cooked with coconut, mustard, and curry leaves—no onion or garlic—served with rice.
explanation: Foogath is a simple vegetable stir-fry that keeps beans bright and lightly coconut-sweet. With rice, it becomes a complete gluten-free, Jain-friendly main.
Use tender French beans. Fresh grated coconut is ideal; frozen grated coconut works when thawed.
instructions: Heat oil. Add mustard seeds; when they pop, add optional urad dal, dried chilli, hing, and curry leaves.
Add beans, turmeric, and salt. Stir well.
Cover and cook on low heat until beans are tender-crisp, 8–10 minutes, sprinkling a little water only if needed.
Stir in grated coconut and cook 1–2 minutes uncovered. Serve over rice with lemon.
stepNotes: Chop beans small so they cook quickly and evenly.
Keep some bite; overcooked foogath tastes dull.
Coconut goes in at the end so it stays fragrant.
Skip urad dal if you do not have it—the dish still works.
A complete plate can add a small bowl of sambar or plain dal when extra protein is needed.

variationSections.title: Vegetable swaps
items: Use cabbage or ivy gourd (tindora) with the same tempering.
Mix beans with peas for color and sweetness.
Cluster beans can replace French beans if you prefer a stronger flavor (different recipe experience).

variationSections.title: Plate adjustments
items: Brown rice or millets instead of white rice.
Add steamed toor dal on the side for more protein.
Serve with cucumber slices or cut fruit for more produce.
